{"step_by_step_thinking": "Vancomycin is a glycopeptide antibiotic that is highly effective against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA). Cefaclor is a beta-lactam antibiotic (cephalosporin) that is generally not effective against MRSA. Cotrimoxazole, which is a combination of trimethoprim and sulfamethoxazole, is effective against MRSA. Ciprofloxacin is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic that is also effective against MRSA. Therefore, the drug that is not useful for MRSA is Cefaclor.", "answer_choice": "A"}
